nodes.title: Landscape Planning Global
nodes.description: Provides a comprehensive understanding of land use planning in a changing world. Students will be working on two projects that could enable communities and regions to thrive, exploring the driving forces of change in the landscape and developing strategic plans for addressing international challenges. Explores the intersection of landscape planning and international challenges, such as climate crisis, global economies, and biodiversity preservation. Prerequisites: ALE 2371 or NR 2430; minimum Sophomore standing; or Instructor permission. Pre/Co-requisites: ALE 2371, NR 2430.
nodes.other_requirements: minimum Sophomore standing; or Instructor permission.

nodes.title: In:Greenhouse Operations &Mgmt
nodes.description: On-site supervised work experience combined with a structured academic learning plan directed by a faculty member or a faculty-staff team in which a faculty member is the instructor of record, for which academic credit is awarded.  Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required

nodes.title: IS:ScientificIllustrationStudy
nodes.description: A course which is tailored to fit the interests of a specific student, which occurs outside the traditional classroom/laboratory setting under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students by permission and override. Instructor permission required.

nodes.title: Greenhouse Operations & Mgmt
nodes.description: Principles and practices of commercial greenhouse management including construction, heating, cooling, container media, watering, fertilization, light and temperature, growth regulators, integrated pest management and disease control. Prerequisite: ALE 1100, ALE 1210, one semester of Biology, or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission. Students must attend both lecture (TR) and lab (M). Open to Degree and PACE Students.

nodes.title: Agroecol, Food Sov. & Soc Mov.
nodes.description: Investigates social, political, and economic elements of the global food system from multiple perspectives, considering the ability to scale-up agroecology, and the potential intersection between agroecology, food sovereignty and government policies.

nodes.title: Exploring Biology 1
nodes.description: Exploring biology from cells to organisms. Topics include origins of life, ancestral organisms, uni- and multi- cellular energetics, evolution of respiration and metabolism, and the genetic code. Credit not awarded for both BCOR 1400 and BIOL 1400 or BCOR 1425.

nodes.title: CL:Sustainable Community Dev
nodes.description: Introduction to perspectives and methods used to develop healthy communities that are economically, socially, and environmentally sustainable with rural and urban, US and international examples. Prerequisites: CDAE 1020, ENVS 1510, or Instructor permission. Cross-listed with: ENVS 2470.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission

nodes.title: Natural Hist & Human Ecology 2
nodes.description: Integrates the science of ecological sciences and the science of humans and society to understand the relationship between the natural landscape's effects on society and social organization, and society's effects on the natural landscape. Pre/Co-requisite: NR 1010.
nodes.other_requirements: Restricted to RSENR students only; Must register for a NR lab A01- A15; Course Fee: $110 field trips

nodes.title: Diversified Farm Planning
nodes.description: Students study diverse farming systems to gain financial, management, and technical knowledge to plan a new or evaluate and existing farm enterprise. Prerequisites: ALE 1210 and one 2000-level ALE course, equivalent experience, or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: equivalent experience, or Instructor permission. Prerequisite enforced by the system: ALE/PSS 1210; Also Acceptable: one 2000-level ALE/PSS course, or equivalent experience, or instructor permission

nodes.title: Agroecology Grad Capstone
nodes.description: The capstone designed for the application of newly developed knowledge and skills in a culminating experience/project that addresses an agroecological topic relevant to the individual student. Prerequisites: ALE 6110, ALE 6120, ALE 6130, ALE 6140.
nodes.other_requirements: instructor permission; Asynchronous Online Course; Open to degree and PACE students

nodes.title: CL:Weed Ecology & Management
nodes.description: Identification, ecology, and management of weeds and other invasive plants in agriculture, urban/suburban landscapes, and natural areas. Prerequisite: ALE 1100, ALE 1210, PBIO 1040, or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission. Prereqs enforced by the system: ALE/PSS 1100or1210orPBIO 1040Open to Degree and PACE students.

nodes.title: Garden Flowers
nodes.description: Outdoor flowers, culture, related topics. Prerequisite: ALE 1100, ALE 1210, one semester of Biology, or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: One semester of Biology, or Instructor permission. Prerequisites enforced by the system: ALE/PSS 1100or1210; or one semester of Biology; Contact Leonard.Perry@uvm.edu for override; Asynchronous online course; Open to degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Prof Dev: Ag, Landscape, & Env
nodes.description: Students will develop and articulate a professional philosophy and improve skills in career development including writing, resume preparation, effective interviewing and negotiation. Prerequisite: Ecological Agriculture, Sustainable Landscape Horticulture, or Agroecology & Landscape Design Major; minimum Sophomore standing; or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: minimum Sophomore standing; or Instructor permission. Sophomore, Junior, or Senior standing; ALD, AGR, ELPD majors or instructor permission. Open to Degree and PACE Students

nodes.title: Quantitative Thinking Life Sci
nodes.description: See Schedule of Courses for specific titles.
nodes.other_requirements: See Schedule of Courses for specific titles. Open to Degree and PACE students.

nodes.title: Commercial Plant Propagation
nodes.description: Principles and practices involved in propagating herbaceous and woody plants by seeds, division, layering, cuttings, budding, grafting, and aseptic tissue culture and micropropagation. Prerequisite: PBIO 1040 or ALE 1100, ALE 1210 or ALE 1370 or BIOL 1400 or BIOL 1450 or BCOR 1400 or BCOR 1450 or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission. Must register for lab ALE 2380 A01 or A02; Also acceptable: One semester Bio, or instructor permission; Contact the instructor for override: mark.starrett@uvm.edu

nodes.title: Intro to Agroecology
nodes.description: Analyzes factors driving current agricultural production systems, the problems associated with the industrial agriculture model, and the variety of approaches and practices for producing food in an ecologically sound and socially just manner.

nodes.title: Professional Skills Colloquium
nodes.description: Presentation and peer review of oral and written communication. Professional development skills including technical writing, literature review, mentorship, scientific integrity, grant proposals, and job market.

nodes.title: Drafting Design I
nodes.description: Creating pictorial presentation and 3D model drawings using computer software. Basic methods and procedures of architectural, three-view, oblique, isometric, and perspective computer-aided drawings.

nodes.title: Ecology, Ecosystems & Environ
nodes.description: Major ecological concepts and their application. Analysis of form, structure, and function of organisms, populations, communities, ecosystems, and landscapes. Prerequisites: RSENR students or Agroecology or Landscape Design or Environmental Studies majors; minimum Sophomore standing.
nodes.other_requirements: minimum Sophomore standing

nodes.title: Solutions in Enviro Studies
nodes.description: Analysis and critique of grand challenges in environmental studies with an emphasis on understanding and solving pervasive global and local environmental problems such as global climate change.
nodes.other_requirements: Must register for an ENVS 1510 A01 - A03 discussion section; Fee: $15.00; Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Permaculture
nodes.description: Design of agriculturally productive environments that have the diversity, stability, and resilience of the natural biosphere to harmoniously integrate landscape and people. Prerequisites: ALE 1100, ALE 1210, BIOL 1450, NR 2030, BCOR 1450, BCOR 2100, or a basic ecology course. Cross-listed with: ENVS 2654.
nodes.other_requirements: minimum sophomore standing

nodes.title: SL:Advanced Agroecology
nodes.description: An in-depth overview of research and applications in the field of agroecology, including current ecological and social dynamics in agricultural landscapes in Vermont and abroad. Prerequisites: ALE 1210 or one semester ecology at the 2000-level or above or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: one semester ecology at the 2000-level or above or Instructor permission. Prereqs enforced by system: PSS/ALE 12101 semester ecology at or above the2000-level or permission; Must register for ALE 3120 lab; Field trip fee: $30. Open to degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Planting Design Studio
nodes.description: Offers practical tools and theoretical frameworks for developing planting design methodologies, emphasizing the cultural and environmental aspects of plant communities. Through lectures, field trips, and assignments, students will explore and apply principles of planting design, focusing on spatial structure, plant performance, ecological value, cultural significance, ornamental qualities, and technical considerations for installation and maintenance. Prerequisites: ALE 2370, ALE 2371, ALE 2230, and ALE 2250; minimum Junior standing; or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: minimum Junior standing; or Instructor permission. Instructor Permission Required Open to Degree and PACE Students;

nodes.title: Exploring Biology 2
nodes.description: An evolutionary perspective to exploring biology. Topics include: patterns of inheritance, Darwinian evolution, evolution of biodiversity, ecology of organisms, human effects on biological systems. Credit not awarded for both BCOR 1450 and BIOL 1450.

nodes.title: W: Pollinators and Perennials
nodes.description: See Schedule of Courses for specific titles.

nodes.title: Dendrology
nodes.description: Classification, silvical characteristics, and identification features of native and introduced trees and shrubs.

nodes.title: Pesticide Application Training
nodes.description: See Schedule of Courses for specific titles.
nodes.other_requirements: instructor permission

nodes.title: Seminar Series
nodes.description: Presentations of personal research by faculty, Graduate students, and outside guest speakers. Attendance and oral presentations are required of Graduate students in Plant & Soil Science. Repeatable two times for Master's students and four times for Doctoral students.

nodes.title: Sust Veg Crops Production
nodes.description: Introduces students to current practices in organic and conventional vegetable cropping systems and farm management. Prerequisite: ALE 1100, ALE 1210, or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission. Open to Degree and PACE Students

nodes.title: Natural Hist & Human Ecology 1
nodes.description: Integrates the science of ecology and the science of humans and society to understand the relationship between the natural landscape's effects on society and social organization, and society's effects on the natural landscape.

nodes.title: Woody Landscape Plants
nodes.description: Identification, climatic requirements, cultural management, and use of woody ornamental plants (trees, shrubs, groundcovers and vines/climbers) in managed landscape settings. Prerequisites: PBIO 1040 or ALE 1100, or ALE 1210, or ALE 1370 or BIOL 1400 or BIOL 1450 or BCOR 1400 or BCOR 1450 or FOR 1210 or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission; 1 semester of Biology or permission; Open to Degree and PACE students; Contact instructor for override: mark.starrett@uvm.edu

nodes.title: Home & Garden Horticulture
nodes.description: Planning, selecting, and maintaining indoor plants, shrubs, trees, flowers, lawns, fruits, herbs and vegetables in and around the home. Plant propagation, as well as pests/diseases and their control, are also covered. This is a course with practical applications and is suitable for students in any major.

nodes.title: Transformative Agroecology
nodes.description: Covers the evolution of agroecology from its origins to the present, including the myriad ways it is both understood and practiced, evaluating examples from around the world to explore agroecology's biophysical, sociocultural, and political potential for food system transformation.

nodes.title: Fundamentals of Soil Science
nodes.description: Biological, chemical, and physical properties of the dynamic soil system as related to plant growth and environmental problems. Prerequisites: ALE 1100, ALE 1210, ALE 1370, ENSC 1010, or CE 1000.
nodes.other_requirements: Inorganic chemistry or permission; Must register for ALE 2610 lab; Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: SL:Landscape Design Studio
nodes.description: Introduces students to the practice and profession of landscape design through studio-based learning. Through a series of design exercises, field experiences, and a real-world final project, students engage the landscape design process from site inventory and analysis to conceptual design, schematic planning, and supporting drawings. Students learn drafting and illustration techniques for design communication, with an emphasis on hand-drawn methods. Prerequisites: Minimum Sophomore standing; ALE 2370, ALE 2560, ARTS 1010, CDAE 1160, or Instructor permission. Co-requisite: ALE 2370.
nodes.other_requirements: Minimum Sophomore standing; Instructor permission. ALE 2370 may be taken concurrently, but students will need to request an override. Open to Degree and PACE students.

nodes.title: UGR: Insect Agroeco andEvolRes
nodes.description: Undergraduate student work on individual or small team research projects under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required; Available credits: 1-3

nodes.title: Principles of Biology 2
nodes.description: Principles of organismal biology; nature of scientific inquiry, plant form and function, pollination ecology, animal phylogeny illustrated by comparative anatomy and physiology; animal behavior. Credit not awarded for both BIOL 1450 and BCOR 1450.
nodes.other_requirements: ASCI, BIOL, BISC, MGEN, MICR, NEUR, PBIO, ZOOL majors should register for BCOR 1450; Degree students only even after level restrictions removed; Post Bac Pre med students register for BIOL 1450

nodes.title: Home & Garden Horticulture Lab
nodes.description: Provides students with basic horticultural skills to enable them to have a better understanding of the care and use of plants in and around the home including turf, ornamental flowers, trees and shrubs, vegetables, herbs and home garden fruit plants. Plant identification, propagation and pest/disease control are also covered. Pre/Co-requisite: ALE 1100.
nodes.other_requirements: Co-req:ALE 1100; Open to Degree and PACE students; Lab fee: $15

nodes.title: Soil Fertility & Conservation
nodes.description: An ecological approach to soil management including nutrient supply and uptake, rhizosphere-microbial interactions, soil conservation, and nutrient management strategies. Prerequisite: ALE 2610 or Instructor permission. Cross-listed with: ENVS 2656.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission

nodes.title: Ecological Foundations of Agro
nodes.description: Examines the ecological foundations of agroecology, largely from a biophysical perspective. Over the course of three sequential modules, students will explore the fundamental principles of ecology and their application to agricultural systems and landscapes. Prerequisite: ALE 6110 or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission; Graduate standing

nodes.title: Plant Pathology
nodes.description: Introduction to the causes of agricultural and forest plant diseases including examination of the relationship of the plant, pathogen, and environment in disease development and disease management. Prerequisite: PBIO 1040, or BIOL 1400 and BIOL 1450, or BCOR 1400 and BCOR 1450, or BCOR 1425, or Instructor permission. Cross-listed with: PBIO 2170.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission

nodes.title: W: Pest Management for Garden
nodes.description: See Schedule of Courses for specific titles.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to Degree and Pace students

nodes.title: Int:Biocontrol & Microbiomes
nodes.description: On-site supervised work experience combined with a structured academic learning plan directed by a faculty member or a faculty-staff team in which a faculty member is the instructor of record, for which academic credit is awarded.  Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Junior or Senior SLH majors only; 1-3 credits; Instructor permission; PACE Students by Permission and Override

nodes.title: Biological Control
nodes.description: Describes theory and application of biological control of insects, disease, and weeds. Discuss ecological factors that contribute to the success of classical, augmentative, and conservation approaches to biological control. Prerequisite: Course in entomology, ecology, or relevant experience.
nodes.other_requirements: Relevant experience

nodes.title: Communication Design I
nodes.description: Directed projects which explore the elements and principles of communication design. Design research, process, experimentation, and production in hand-based and computer-generated design application for multi-modal presentations. Prerequisite: Community Development & Applied Economics major or minor; or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Community Development & Applied Economics major or minor; or Instructor permission; Applied Design Minor only, or instructor permission. Required materials for this course are a subscription to the Adobe Suite. Recommended prerequisite: CDAE 1150, Visual Communication

nodes.title: Ecology and Evolution
nodes.description: Ecosystem and community structure, population growth, species interactions and niche dynamics, population and chromosomal genetics, speciation in fossil records, ecology of animal behavior, applied ecology. Prerequisites: BCOR 1400, BIOL 1400, or BIOL 1000; BCOR 1450, BIOL 1450, BIOL 1005, or BCOR 1425; MATH 1212 or MATH 1234.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students with permission and override.

nodes.title: SL:Ecological Landscape Design
nodes.description: Studio course synthesizing work from fields of landscape ecology and landscape design, exploring ecological design alternatives at multiple scales, and developing multifunctional landscape solutions. Prerequisites: Junior standing; ALE 2370, ALE 2371; or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Junior standing; Instructor permission.

nodes.title: Intro Environmental Sci
nodes.description: Explores the complex interactions between humans and environmental systems and the ecological foundations and scientific principles to better understand how the coupled human-natural system works, and how science can be used to help solve environmental problems.

nodes.title: Intro to Geog Info Systems
nodes.description: Understanding and application of computer-based, geographically-referenced information systems. Prerequisite: Minimum Sophomore standing.
nodes.other_requirements: Minimum Sophomore standing. Must register for a NR 2430 lab A01 - A06

nodes.title: Being a TA: Reflective Practic
nodes.description: Student service as a teaching assistant, usually in an introductory level course in the discipline, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: A Bug's Life
nodes.description: An introduction to the world of insects and their impact on our everyday lives, from the food we eat to solving murder crimes.

nodes.title: Teaching Assistantship
nodes.description: Undergraduate student service as a teaching assistant, usually in an introductory level course in the discipline, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Indoor Plants
nodes.description: Indoor flowers, culture, related topics such as design. Prerequisite: ALE 1100, ALE 1210, one semester of Biology, or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission. Contact Leonard.Perry@uvm.edu for override.

nodes.title: UGR:Design Research in Agroeco
nodes.description: Undergraduate student work on individual or small team research projects under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required; Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Sustainable Orchard Management
nodes.description: Principles and practices of commercial tree fruit production, including site considerations; cultivars; training; nutrient, water and pest management; harvest and postharvest considerations. Special emphasis on environmental and economic sustainability of the orchard system. Pre/Co-requisites: ALE 1100, ALE 1210, BIOL 1400, BIOL 1450, BCOR 1400, or BCOR 1450; ALE 2610.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to Degree and Pace students.

nodes.title: Lab TA Woody Landscape Plants
nodes.description: Undergraduate student service as a teaching assistant, usually in an introductory level course in the discipline, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required; PACE Student by Permission and Override.

nodes.title: Entomology & Pest Mgmt
nodes.description: Covers basic entomology, insect diversity and identification, and the basic principles of pest management. Prerequisite: BIOL 1400 and BIOL 1450, or BCOR 1400 and BCOR 1450, or BCOR 1425.
nodes.other_requirements: Permission; Minimum Sophomore standing; Must register for ALE 2060 A01 or ALE 2060 A02 lab.

nodes.title: Accelerated Biology
nodes.description: Selected topics from the full year of introductory biology, compressed into one semester. For students with demonstrated mastery of basic biology (e.g., AP credit). Permission required. Credit not awarded for both BCOR 1425 and BCOR 1400 or BIOL 1400. Pre/co-requisite: CHEM 1400, CHEM 1405, or CHEM 1410.
nodes.other_requirements: For students with demonstrated mastery of basic biology (e.g., AP credit). Permission required. Credit not awarded for both BCOR 1425 and BCOR 1400 or BIOL 1400. By invitation only; for FTFY students with AP score of 4. Instructor permission required. Must register for a BCOR 1425 lab. Must be enrolled in CHEM 1400 if have no CHEM credit.

nodes.title: Principles of Biology 1
nodes.description: Principles of cellular biochemistry; cell biology; genetics and evolution. Topics: biochemistry; metabolism, cell structure/function; respiration; photosynthesis; molecular, Mendelian and population genetics; genetics of evolution. Credit not awarded for both BIOL 1400 and BCOR 1400 or BCOR 1425.
nodes.other_requirements: Credit not awarded for both BIOL 1400 and BCOR 1400 or BCOR 1425. Must register for a BIOL 1400 lab; Lab supply fee: $30; Degree students only even after level restrictions removed; Post Bac Pre Med & PACE students register for BIOL 1400 ZRA Colocated with BIOL 1400 ZRA

nodes.title: Intro to Ecological Planning
nodes.description: Explores conservation planning and design strategies for restoring healthy ecosystems and building healthy livable communities. Through lectures, guest speakers, case studies, book discussions, field trips, and real-world class projects, students are given hands-on opportunities to learn about ecological planning in Vermont and beyond.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to degree and PACE students; Restrict to first and second year students only (or instructor permission)

nodes.title: Urban Landscape Design Studio
nodes.description: A comprehensive understanding of landscape design strategies in the built environment. Students will work on one project that could enable communities and cities to thrive, exploring the driving forces of change in the landscape and developing strategic masterplans for addressing socioecological challenges. Explores the intersection of urbanism and landscape design, such as climate crisis, local economies, biodiversity preservation, environmental justice, and social equity. Prerequisites: ALE 2371 or CDAE 1010; minimum Sophomore standing; or Instructor permission. Pre/Co-requisites: ALE 2371 or CDAE 1010; Sophomore standing.
nodes.other_requirements: minimum Sophomore standing; or Instructor permission. Prereqs enforced by the system: Sophomore standing. Open to degree and PACE students. A field trip to Montreal, Canada, is mandatory (Early September). A passport or other travel documents are required. Design studio-centered approach. Intermediate to Advanced visual communications skills. Software: Adobe Creative Cloud, SketchUp, and others. Prerequisites: Any of the following courses: Digital Illustration, Drafting Design I, Landscape Design Fundamentals, or Communication Design I.

nodes.title: Graduate Independent Research
nodes.description: Student work on individual or small team research projects under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Master's Thesis Research
nodes.description: Research for Master?s Thesis.

nodes.title: Landscape Construction & Tech
nodes.description: Focuses on constructing and detailing the primary landforms and hardscape components utilized in the landscape design industry. These components include landforms, grading, drainage systems, patios, paths, ramps, bike paths, staircases, retention walls, permeable parking lots, and green infrastructure such as wetlands, bioswales, green roofs, rain barrels, and living walls. Students will use standard tools and REVIT for different fields and computer lab exercises. Prerequisites: ALE 2371 or CDAE 1010; minimum Sophomore standing; or Instructor permission. Pre/Co-requisites: ALE 2371, CDAE 1010.
nodes.other_requirements: minimum Sophomore standing; or Instructor permission.

nodes.title: IS:Adv Landscape Design Studio
nodes.description: A course which is tailored to fit the interests of a specific student, which occurs outside the traditional classroom/laboratory setting under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Illustrating Botanicals
nodes.description: Training in the skills required to produce aesthetically pleasing visual representations of botanical subjects grounded in technically correct plant morphology and anatomy. Use of line, shading, and color explored in depth. Media include graphite, pen and ink, colored pencils and watercolor. Includes a final project. Prerequisite: Junior/Senior Standing.
nodes.other_requirements: Junior/Senior Standing. Open to Degree and PACE students.

nodes.title: Landscape Design Fundamentals
nodes.description: Introduces students to the history and principles of landscape design. Examines various aspects of built environments with consideration of natural and cultural phenomena, with topics to include: spatial scale, equity, land use, design precedent, soils, plants, water, recreation, transportation, and more. Prerequisite: ALE 1370, ALE 1100, ALE 1210, ENVS 1510, ENVS 1520, NR 1010, NR 1020, or CDAE 1010; minimum Sophomore standing; or Instructor permission. Cross-listed with: CDAE 2370, ENVS 2650.
nodes.other_requirements: minimum Sophomore standing; or Instructor permission. Other prereq, not enforced by the system:ENVS 1500; Minimum sophomore standing;

nodes.title: PAR & Transdiscipl Agroecology
nodes.description: Introduces students to Participatory Action Research (PAR) in the context of agroecology, and examines how the integration of PAR and transdisciplinary approaches can serve to deepen our collective understanding of complex problems/issues. Prerequisite: ALE 6110.
nodes.other_requirements: instructor permission

nodes.title: Graduate Independent Research
nodes.description: Student work on individual or small team research projects under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required.

nodes.title: Doctoral Dissertation Research
nodes.description: Research for Doctoral Dissertation.

nodes.title: Intro to Botany
nodes.description: Introduction to plant biology from cells to ecosystems. Explore plant structure and function, evolution, diversity, ecology, and the role of plants in maintaining ecosystem functions and services. Integrates lectures, hands-on labs, and discussion-based activities to build scientific skills, including data analysis, hypothesis testing, and scientific communication. Provides foundational knowledge for advanced study in the plant and life sciences.
nodes.other_requirements: Must register for PBIO 1040 lab A01 - A05

nodes.title: Soil/Water Pollution/Bioremed
nodes.description: Examines key issues in pollution of soil and water. Topics include type of pollutants, their reactions in soil and water, pollution prevention and bioremediation. Prerequisites: ALE 2610 or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission. Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: IS:Nature-Base Landscp Design
nodes.description: A course which is tailored to fit the interests of a specific student, which occurs outside the traditional classroom/laboratory setting under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE Students by Permission and Override.

nodes.title: Agricultural Policy and Ethics
nodes.description: An examination of American agriculture and policies from various perspectives - historical, political, ecological, technological, social, economic, and ethical. Emphasis on contemporary issues, policy options, and future development. Prerequisites: CDAE 2020, ALE 3120, or equivalent. Cross-listed with: CDAE 3080.
nodes.other_requirements: equivalent
